---
title: "No GRUB after installing 12.10"
date: 2012-12-27
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by colmeweb on 2012-12-27
I just finished installing 12.10 64bit on a toshiba satellite c850d that had Windows 8 pre-installed. I was going to run it as a dual boot but the grub menu never pops up. The computer boots into Ubuntu, and I can access the windows files from Ubuntu, but there is no option to log into windows. I've looked around but none of the information stands out as overly helpful. If anyone can point me towards the answer, or just tell me what to do that would be great. 

Thanks.

---

### Post by Luigi2012SM64DS on 2012-12-27
I think the command is "sudo grub-install /dev/sda"

---

### Post by colmeweb on 2012-12-27
No joy on sudo grub-install /dev/sda thanks anyway Luigi

---

### Post by oldfred on 2012-12-27
If you had Windows 8 pre-installed you have secure boot and UEFI.

Did you install Ubuntu 12.10 64 bit version in UEFI mode or BIOS mode?

If in BIOS mode Boot-Repair can convert by un-installing grub-pc (for BIOS boot) and installing grub-efi (for efi boot). 

Both Windows and Ubuntu have to be either UEFI/efi or BIOS boot mode.

       Post the link to the BootInfo report that this creates. Is part of Boot-Repair:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Info](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Info)
Boot Repair -Also handles LVM, GPT, separate /boot and UEFI dual boot.:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Repair](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Boot-Repair)
You can repair many boot issues with this or 'Create BootInfo' report (Other Options) & post the link it creates, so we can see your exact configuration and diagnose advanced problems.
Install in Ubuntu liveCD or USB or Full RepairCD with Boot-Repair (for newer computers) 
[http://sourceforge.net/p/boot-repair/home/Home/](http://sourceforge.net/p/boot-repair/home/Home/)
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UbuntuSecureRemix](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UbuntuSecureRemix)

    
       You will need to use the 64 bit version of 12.10 and from the UEFI menu boot the flash drive in UEFI mode. That way it will install in UEFI mode.
Systems need quick boot or fast boot turned off in UEFI settings.
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UEFI](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UEFI)
[https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/UEFI](https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/UEFI)

---

### Post by colmeweb on 2012-12-27
So I ran a scan with boot repair and then ran the recommended repairs.
When I restarted the computer I was met with the message 
cannot find boot/grub.i386-pc/normal.mod 
so now the only way my computer works is through the live usb.

The message 
Please do not forget to make your BIOS boot on sda2/EFI/Ubuntu/grubx64.efi file
but I have no idea how to do that.

And the logs from boot repair are at these links.
Log:  [http://paste.ubuntu.com/1470789/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/1470789/)

Repair: [http://paste.ubuntu.com/1470811/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/1470811/)


[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG]
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG]

### Post by oldfred on 2012-12-27
Boot-Repair converted you from BIOS based boot to UEFI based boot and now the BIOS based does not work as expected.

You have to go into your UEFI/BIOS menu and choose ubuntu as boot choice. Many computers flash a UEFI/BIOS screen for a few seconds and tell you which keys to use to boot. It varies by vendor.

Microsoft published a list of BIOS boot keys.
       UEFI/BIOS Boot keys - about halfway down on this page
[http://social.technet.microsoft.com/wiki/contents/articles/12911.tips-for-configuring-your-bios-settings-to-work-with-windows-to-go.aspx](http://social.technet.microsoft.com/wiki/contents/articles/12911.tips-for-configuring-your-bios-settings-to-work-with-windows-to-go.aspx)

    
You did turn off fast boot in your UEFI/BIOS first? Some have reported major issues if they have not done that.

---

### Post by colmeweb on 2012-12-27
When I switched my computer back to UEFI the grub menu did show up, but neither my installed Ubuntu or the live usb will load. widnows works again, but nothing ells. According to ubuntu's site the newer 64bit downloads should work. 
I grabbed a picture of the grub settings for ubuntu, but I can't post it or really describe what it says.

### Post by colmeweb on 2012-12-27
So after running boot repair my ubuntu system was completely unacceptable. I have just re-installed the whole system and for now I am just going to run ubuntu only and hope that I don't need windows for anything in the near future.

As before I installed the ubuntu-secure-remix-12.10-64bit file. If anyone knows where I can file an official statement that would be great. This is the type of thing that would scare away just about every first time user.

Thanks to everyone that tried to help.

---

### Post by oldfred on 2012-12-27
You have to file a bug report with the documentation of what the system was.
       bug reports info on how to do:
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/ReportingBugs](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/ReportingBugs)

    
Someone who got it to work.
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2098753](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2098753)
